title: MotionLinkInput Object
description: Defines all of the information required to create a new MotionLink. This object provides equivalent functionality to the MotionLink command dialog in that it gathers the required information to create a MotionLink.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isReversed
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the direction of the motion is reversed or not.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: jointOne
properties.description: Gets and sets the first Joint or AsBuiltJoint for this MotionLink. When you link two motions from the same joint, a valid joint should have its joint motion type of BallJointType, CylindricalJointType, PinSlotJointType or PlanarJointType. A joint whose joint motion is a RigidJointMotion type is never valid as the first joint..

properties.name: jointTwo
properties.description: Gets and sets the second Joint or AsBuiltJoint for this MotionLink. This can be a joint or null, when this is set to null then the two motions are from the same joint specified by jointOne. A joint whose joint motion is a RigidJointMotion type is never valid as the second joint.

properties.name: motionOne
properties.description: Gets and sets the first motion type.

properties.name: motionTwo
properties.description: Gets and sets the second motion type.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: valueOne
properties.description: Gets and sets the first motion link value. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters for length and radians for angle. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in" or "60 deg") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length or angle.

properties.name: valueTwo
properties.description: Gets and sets the second motion link value. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters for length and radians for angle. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in" or "60 deg") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length or angle.

classType.title: MotionLinkInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isReversed.title: MotionLinkInput.isReversed Property
isReversed.description: Gets and sets whether the direction of the motion is reversed or not.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = motionLinkInput_var.isReversed

# Set the value of the property.
motionLinkInput_var.isReversed = propertyValue

propertyValue = motionLinkInput_var.isValid
